content.full_text:
May 11, 1992

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

RE: FILING DEADLINES FOR QUALIFICATION REPORTS
GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 84200.5(f)

Dear Scott:

Government Code section 84200.5(f) was amended in 1991. It now provides as follows:

Committees formed or existing primarily to support or oppose the qualification of a ballot measure and proponents of a state ballot measure who control a committee formed or existing primarily to support the qualification of a measure, shall file a campaign statement 21 days after the deadline for filing petitions. The closing date for the period covered by a statement required by this subdivision shall be seven days prior to the deadline for filing the statement.

Before this section was amended, this reporting requirement was triggered by either the date petitions were filed or the filing deadline, whichever was earlier. Now the requirement is tied only to the filing deadline for petitions.

I am writing to request clarification of this requirement. Under the Elections Code, there are statutory deadlines for filing initiative petitions. Unless petitions are filed by the deadline, the petition is considered void for all purposes. Some of the applicable deadlines are as follows:

Statewide initiatives - 150 days after the official summary date (Elections Code §3513)

County initiatives - 180 days after the receipt of the title and summary or after termination of any action for writ of mandate (Elections Code § 3705)

District initiatives - 180 days after the date of publication or posting (Elections Code § 5152.5)

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
May 11, 1992
Page 2

Municipal initiatives - 180 days after the date of receipt of the title and summary (Elections Code § 4006)

In addition to these deadlines, there are also deadlines for the certification of an statewide initiative for a particular ballot (e.g., June 23, 1992 for the November 3, 1992 ballot). With respect to statewide initiatives, the Secretary of State also imposes a deadline for filing petitions which will ensure that the initiative will be certified in time for the next statewide ballot.

We would like your guidance on exactly which filing deadlines trigger the report required under § 84200.5(f).

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL,
FONG & LEIDIGH

[signature]
DIANE M. FISHBURN
DMF/CCZ
